concerns.text: Spec US-1/US-2 acceptance scenarios mandate 1000 games per condition. Tasks T016/T021 implement 500 games ('500 games per Plan; Spec N=1000 noted'). This silently weakens the statistical power and violates the acceptance criteria without explicit spec amendment.

Spec FR-009 requires power analysis with N=1000 games. Task T026 references 'N=500 per Plan; Spec N=1000 flagged'. The task implements the weaker plan value rather than the spec requirement, weakening the power analysis constraint.

Constitution Principle VII requires 'report performance curves for both the specialization index and cue‑retrieval efficiency' across window sizes. Task T025 implements the token sweep but does not explicitly ensure performance curves are output, risking semantic weakening of the robustness requirement.
